title: MigrationPresentation (v1)
description: Agent-signed envelope for migration. Bundles MigrationToken + DepartureAttestation + CredentialBundle + identity file. Destination relays validate per spec §8.2; if accepted, the agent's motebit_id is preserved across the relay change. Foundation law §8.4: acceptance is a local admission decision; relays MAY decline. See spec/migration-v1.md §8.1.

token_id.description: UUIDv7 identifier for this specific authorization. Stable through expiry.

motebit_id.description: Agent's motebit identity (UUIDv7) authorized to migrate.

source_relay_url.description: Issuing relay's canonical URL. Lets destination relays reach back to verify chain-of-custody if needed.

issued_at.description: Unix timestamp in milliseconds when the source relay signed the token.

expires_at.description: Unix timestamp in milliseconds when the token becomes invalid. Default per spec: 72 hours from issuance. Source relay MUST NOT revoke before expiry except on agent-initiated cancellation (foundation law §4.3).

suite.description: Cryptosuite identifier. Always `motebit-jcs-ed25519-b64-v1` for migration artifacts: JCS canonicalization (RFC 8785), Ed25519 primitive, base64url signature, hex public key. Verifiers reject missing or unknown values fail-closed.

signature.description: Base64url-encoded Ed25519 signature over `canonicalJson(body)` where `body` = the artifact minus `signature`. Signed by the source relay (NOT the agent).

migration_token.description: Source-relay-signed authorization to migrate. Verifier checks the source relay's signature on this token first — without authorization, the rest is irrelevant.

motebit_id.description: Agent's motebit identity being attested for.

first_seen.description: Unix timestamp in milliseconds when the agent first registered at this relay.

last_active.description: Unix timestamp in milliseconds of the agent's last task or interaction.

trust_level.description: Agent's trust level at departure. Free-form string per spec; conventional values include `untrusted`, `verified`, `trusted`, `revoked` — the destination relay applies its own trust mapping.

successful_tasks.description: Total completed tasks the agent executed as worker (≥ 0).

failed_tasks.description: Total failed tasks the agent executed as worker (≥ 0).

credentials_issued.description: Total credentials this relay issued to the agent (≥ 0).

balance_at_departure.description: Virtual account balance in micro-units (1 USD = 1,000,000) at attestation time. Lets destination relays understand the economic state the agent is bringing across.

attested_at.description: Unix timestamp in milliseconds when this attestation was signed.

suite.description: Cryptosuite identifier. Always `motebit-jcs-ed25519-b64-v1` for migration artifacts: JCS canonicalization (RFC 8785), Ed25519 primitive, base64url signature, hex public key. Verifiers reject missing or unknown values fail-closed.

signature.description: Base64url-encoded Ed25519 signature over `canonicalJson(body)` where `body` = the artifact minus `signature`. Signed by the source relay. Foundation law (§5.3): the relay MUST NOT fabricate or inflate attestation data.

departure_attestation.description: Source-relay-signed history snapshot. Verifier checks the source relay's signature; the trust level + task counts inform the destination's admission decision.

motebit_id.description: Agent's motebit identity (UUIDv7). The signer of the bundle. Destination relays verify the embedded signature against the public key bound to this identity at the source.

exported_at.description: Unix timestamp in milliseconds when the agent emitted the bundle. Lets destination relays detect stale exports (e.g. replayed migrations).

items.description: Arbitrary JSON object — its shape is defined by a separate wire format (credential@1.0 for `credentials`, credential-anchor@1.0 for `anchor_proofs`, identity@1.0 for `key_succession`). The bundle's signature covers the bytes; validate inner shape against the dedicated schemas after bundle verification.

credentials.description: W3C Verifiable Credential 2.0 documents the agent has accumulated. Empty array is valid (a fresh agent with no issued credentials). Each entry validates against credential@1.0 (W3C VC 2.0 + motebit's eddsa-jcs-2022 cryptosuite profile).

anchor_proofs.description: On-chain credential anchor proofs (Merkle inclusion + chain reference). Lets the destination relay verify which credentials were anchored to which chain transaction without reaching out to the chain. Each entry validates against credential-anchor@1.0.

key_succession.description: Full key rotation history. Required so the destination relay can verify the agent's current public key chains back to the originally-anchored identity. Each entry validates against identity@1.0.

bundle_hash.description: SHA-256 hex digest of the canonical JSON of all fields EXCEPT `bundle_hash` and `signature`. Lets consumers verify the bundle's content-addressing is consistent with the embedded signature without recomputing both.

suite.description: Cryptosuite identifier. Always `motebit-jcs-ed25519-b64-v1` for credential bundles today: JCS canonicalization (RFC 8785), Ed25519 signature, base64url-encoded signature, hex-encoded public key. Verifiers reject missing or unknown values fail-closed.

signature.description: Base64url-encoded Ed25519 signature over `canonicalJson(body)` where `body` = this object minus `signature`. Verify with the agent's public key (resolved via `key_succession` for the timestamp `exported_at`).

credential_bundle.description: Agent-signed export of portable reputation. Verifier checks the agent's signature; per-entry credentials + anchor proofs validate against their dedicated schemas.

identity_file.description: Full motebit.md content as a string — the agent's identity@1.0 document. Lets the destination relay verify the agent's current public key chains back to the originally-anchored identity.

presented_at.description: Unix timestamp in milliseconds when the agent assembled and signed the presentation.

suite.description: Cryptosuite identifier. Always `motebit-jcs-ed25519-b64-v1` for migration artifacts: JCS canonicalization (RFC 8785), Ed25519 primitive, base64url signature, hex public key. Verifiers reject missing or unknown values fail-closed.

signature.description: Base64url-encoded Ed25519 signature over `canonicalJson(body)` where `body` = the artifact minus `signature`. Signed by the agent. Binds the four nested artifacts together — tampering with any nested artifact breaks this outer signature.
